text: Common $o$-vinylphenols give the formal (5 + 2) cycloaddition products with alkynes in the presence of catalytic amounts of [Cp*RhCl2]2 and Cu(OAc)2; the terminal C–H of the alkenyl unit is selectively activated towards Rh and subsequently cleaved. The protocol uses readily available starting materials for the formal preparation of valuable heterocycles like benzoxepines in a highly efficient and atom-economical fashion. When the alkyne is substituted with CO, a formal (5 + 1) cycloaddition affords coumarin derivatives. The procedure is amongst the few instances of constructing 7-membered heterocycles by utilizing C–H bond functionalization in common chemistry and utilizing relatively simple starting materials.
